:PROPERTIES:
:ID:       ffffffff-ffff-ffff-ffff-ffffffffffff
:END:
#+title: Anchor playground

A file that exercises every anchor type: `<<dedicated target>>`,
`<<<radio target>>>`, `CUSTOM_ID`, headline title, and free-text search.

* First section

<<named-target>> The named target sits before the paragraph it labels.

Body after named target — lorem ipsum dolor sit amet.

* Second section
:PROPERTIES:
:CUSTOM_ID: second
:END:

The second section's body. The CUSTOM_ID property "second" is the
canonical anchor for this headline.

* Third section with code

#+BEGIN_SRC rust
fn main() {           (ref:entry)
    println!("source block");
}
#+END_SRC

Source blocks are not anchors, but text *inside* them can still be
searched.

* Fourth section with radio target

<<<on-every-occurrence>>> This paragraph follows the radio target.
The radio target is file-local: it points every literal occurrence
of the term at this definition.

* Fifth section with named table

#+NAME: growth-table
| year | nodes |
|------+-------|
| 2023 | 1     |
| 2024 | 2     |

See [[growth-table]] for the data.

The text[fn:1] refers to a footnote.

The text[fn:side-note] also refers to a named footnote.

[fn:1] This is the footnote body.

[fn:side-note] A named footnote body, used for cross-references.
